name: New England Clam Chowder from Fred Goslin
recipeInstructions:
This chowder is not as good as the chowder served at Jay's Diner in Rochester, NY, which is undoubtedly the best I've tasted so far, but it's close...
1. Place bacon in a 2-quart casserole and microwave on high (100%), until bacon is crisp, about 3 to 4 minutes.
2. Add reserved clam juice, potatoes, and onions. Cover and microwave on high (100%) until potatoes are tender, about 8 to 10 minutes, stirring after half the cooking time.
3. Blend in the flour, stir in milk, salt, and pepper. Microwave on high (100%) until thickened, about 4 to 7 minutes, stirring 2 to 3 times during the cooking time.
4. Blend in the reserved clam juice and potatoes, and stir in the clams. Microwave on medium-high (70%) until thickened and heated through, approximately 4 to 5 minutes.
